question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a TOKEN_FREEZE scenario with a high batchDivisor value so that the batchSize in CompositeTransactionGenerator is set to 1, causing freeze transactions to be submitted one at a time with no corresponding batch of unfreeze transactions, making it impossible to maintain a freeze/unfreeze balance?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a TOKEN_FREEZE scenario with logResponse=true so that every freeze response is logged, causing log flooding that degrades system performance and delays TOKEN_UNFREEZE transaction processing on the single-threaded subscribeOn(Schedulers.single()) scheduler, resulting in unfreeze timeouts and permanent fund freezing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodeMaxBackoff property (default 1 minute) in PublishProperties so that when a node is slow to respond to TOKEN_UNFREEZE transactions, the SDK backs off for up to 1 minute per attempt, causing the unfreeze to exceed its timeout while the freeze has already been confirmed, leaving funds permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a TOKEN_FREEZE scenario with duration set to the minimum (30 seconds per ScenarioProperties) and a TOKEN_UNFREEZE scenario with a longer duration, so that the freeze scenario runs to completion and is disabled by CompositeTransactionGenerator.rebuild() before the unfreeze scenario has processed all pending freeze transactions, leaving accounts permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the OBJECT_MAPPER configured with ACCEPT_CASE_INSENSITIVE_PROPERTIES=true and FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES=false in ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.convert() to inject a freezeDefault=true property into a TokenCreateTransactionSupplier via the scenario properties map, causing all newly associated accounts to be frozen by default with no freeze key set, making the freeze permanent?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES=false setting in ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.convert() to inject extra properties into a TokenFreezeTransactionSupplier that override the accountId or tokenId fields via case-insensitive matching (e.g., 'ACCOUNTID' instead of 'accountId'), causing freeze transactions to target unintended accounts and permanently freeze their funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ExpressionConverterImpl.doConvert() method's use of Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...) so that a TOKEN expression that creates a token with freezeDefault=true retries indefinitely, creating multiple tokens all with freeze-by-default behavior, and then the publish() pipeline uses these tokens to freeze all associated accounts permanently?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ConcurrentHashMap expressions cache in ExpressionConverterImpl so that a token expression resolving to a token with freezeDefault=true is cached and reused across all scenarios that reference it, causing every account that associates with that token to be automatically frozen, with the freeze state being permanent because no unfreeze scenario references the same cached token?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FreezeTransactionSupplier's default freezeType of FREEZE_ONLY (set at field initialization, not at get() call time) so that a FREEZE scenario in publish() submits a network-level FREEZE_ONLY transaction that halts all network activity, effectively freezing all token transfers and fund movements permanently until a manual FREEZE_ABORT is submitted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CompositeTransactionGenerator.rebuild() method so that when a TOKEN_UNFREEZE scenario reaches its limit and is disabled via e.getScenario().getProperties().setEnabled(false), the TOKEN_FREEZE scenario continues running because it has a higher limit, causing a net accumulation of frozen accounts with no unfreeze path?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EnumeratedDistribution sampling in CompositeTransactionGenerator.next() so that by setting a very high tps for a TOKEN_FREEZE scenario and a very low tps for a TOKEN_UNFREEZE scenario, the probability distribution causes freeze transactions to be sampled far more frequently than unfreeze transactions, resulting in a net freeze accumulation that permanently locks funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TransactionPublisher.publish() method's use of secureRandom.nextInt(publishProperties.getClients()) for client selection so that TOKEN_FREEZE and TOKEN_UNFREEZE transactions are submitted through different SDK clients with different node routing, causing the freeze to be confirmed on one node while the unfreeze is routed to a different node that hasn't yet propagated the freeze state, resulting in the unfreeze being rejected and funds remaining frozen?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timeout(properties.getTimeout()) call in TransactionPublisher.publish() so that by configuring a TOKEN_UNFREEZE scenario with the minimum timeout of 1 second (DurationMin(seconds=1)), the unfreeze transaction consistently times out before receiving a response, while the freeze transaction with a longer timeout succeeds, leaving funds permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorMap(t -> !(t instanceof PublishException), t -> new PublishException(request, t)) in TransactionPublisher.publish() so that a non-PublishException thrown during TOKEN_UNFREEZE processing is wrapped and then swallowed by the onErrorContinue(PublishException.class, ...) handler in publish(), silently dropping the unfreeze and leaving funds permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeSupplier.get() method's round-robin counter (counter.getAndIncrement() % nodes.size()) so that TOKEN_FREEZE transactions are always routed to fast nodes while TOKEN_UNFREEZE transactions are routed to slow nodes due to the counter state, causing unfreeze transactions to time out while freeze transactions succeed, resulting in permanent fund freezing?
